nameMeanings.name: Levington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name, variant of   Levington  , but this is an E Anglian surname and the name in S and SW England must either reflect migration or have a different origin; see (2).

2 English: locative name, Lewington and Levington are perhaps variant pronunciations of the Wilts surname Lavington . The vowel change is not recorded in the place-name, but there is some evidence for occasional raising of late Middle English [a] to early Modern English [e]. The development of intervocalic [v] to [w] is not uncommon in Middle English dialects, and original -ton is often replaced in surnames with -don and -den. Compare therefore Johannes and Michaelis Lavynton’, 1381 in Poll Tax (Reading, Berks) with Mathew Lewington, 1641 in IGI (Reading, Berks), and Johanne Lavynton’, 1381 in Poll Tax (Hidden, Berks) with Josias Lewendon, 1611 in IGI (Hungerford, Berks); Hidden is in Hungerford parish. Compare also Mary Lewington, 1640, John Laventon, 1705 in IGI (Cheriton, Hants). However, these may be different surnames.

variants.name: Poll Tax
meanings: 1 Irish, Scottish Gaelic: relationship name: apparently a reduced form of an unrecorded Gaelic name, Mac Ruanaidh ‘son of Ruanadh’, a personal name from Gaelic ruanaidh ‘strong man, champion’. The initial C comes from the final consonant of mac. Compare Rooney .

2 English: of uncertain origin. It might be a variant of Crowner ‘coroner’, once a Sussex name, with the final syllable pronounced -ey. Compare Simon Croner, 1296 in Subsidy Rolls (Horton, Tottington, and Edburton, Sussex); Willelmus Croner, 1379 in Poll Tax (South Mundham, Sussex). Alternatively it might be a variant of Corney with the medial -r- metathesized.

variants.name: IGI
meanings: 1 Scottish: perhaps a variant of Kelvey (a reduced form of McKelvey ), for the two names appear in the same parishes in SW Scotland and IoM; compare Jon Kelvie, 1697 in IGI (Lezayre, IoM); Grisal Kelvie, 1777 in IGI (Greenock, Renfrews). However, the acquisition of additional -n is difficult to explain. It may in fact be a reduced form of McKelvin , for which there is little historical evidence. There is no known connection with the River Kelvin or with the modern settlement of Kelvinside in Glasgow.

variants.name: Lavington
meanings: English: locative name from Market and West Lavington (Wilts).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Levington was born in 1236, in Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh FitzRalph de Levington, was 52 and her mother, Agnes de Gresley, was 41. She had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Roland de Carrick. She died about 1307, in her hometown, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda Levington est née en 1236, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh FitzRalph de Levington, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Gresley, avait 41 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Roland de Carrick. Elle est décédée environ 1307, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda Levington 1236 in Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh FitzRalph de Levington 52 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Gresley 41. Sie hatte mit Roland de Carrick mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1307 im Alter von 72 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda Levington è nata il nel 1236 nel Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Hugh FitzRalph de Levington, aveva 52 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Gresley, aveva 41 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Roland de Carrick. Morì circa 1307, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni.
